name: Ermita del Calvario Trail
name_original: Ermita del Calvario – trail run desde Cabeza Del Buey
description: This moderate 3.4-mile trail run from Cabeza Del Buey to Ermita del Calvario offers panoramic views and beautiful sunsets.
region_intro: Jogging around Cabeza Del Buey offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by rolling hills and varied terrain. The region features routes with notable elevation gains, including trails that ascend to points like Cerro Masatrigo and Peñón del Pez. These running routes provide opportunities to explore both botanical areas and historical sites. Best jogging routes around Cabeza Del Buey The most popular jogging route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route features varied terrain with a notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route includes significant climbs, offering panoramic views from higher ground. Local runners also love the Ermita del Calvario Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through a landscape with moderate elevation changes, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging around Cabeza Del Buey is defined by botanical trails, historical landmarks, and varied elevation profiles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 31 runners have used komoot to explore Cabeza Del Buey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The running routes around Cabeza Del Buey are generally challenging. Out of the 7 routes available, 5 are classified as difficult, and 2 are moderate. There are no easy routes listed, indicating that most trails involve significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Cabeza Del Buey area?
A: Yes, several routes in the region are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991754988/penon-del-pez-running-circular-from-penalsordo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 5.4-mile loop offering panoramic views. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 8.3 miles.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Jogging routes in Cabeza Del Buey offer diverse scenery, characterized by rolling hills, varied terrain, and botanical areas. You can expect trails with notable elevation gains, leading to points like Cerro Masatrigo, and paths that explore both natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Many routes offer views of significant natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option, spanning approximately 8.5 miles (13.7 km). Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 8.3 miles (13.4 km) and rated moderate.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Given the region's climate, the best times for running in Cabeza Del Buey are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for physical activity, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: How many running trails are available in the Cabeza Del Buey area?
A: There are 7 distinct running trails available around Cabeza Del Buey, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for runners to explore the region's unique landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: More than 31 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain of Cabeza Del Buey. Users often highlight the challenging nature of the routes, the rewarding panoramic views from higher grounds like Peñón del Pez, and the opportunity to experience both botanical and historical aspects of the region.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes suitable for those looking for less intense challenges?
A: Yes, while many routes are difficult, there are moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992977759/ermita-del-calvario-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Calvario Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.4-mile route with moderate elevation changes. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, which is longer at 8.3 miles but has a moderate difficulty rating.

Q: Can I find routes that offer significant elevation gain for a more intense workout?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Cabeza Del Buey feature notable elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991754988/penon-del-pez-running-circular-from-penalsordo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes significant climbs, offering a strenuous workout and rewarding views from its highest points.

Q: What is the typical length of running routes in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The running routes in Cabeza Del Buey vary in length, generally ranging from around 3.4 miles (5.4 km) to 8.5 miles (13.7 km). This variety allows runners to choose routes that fit their preferred distance and training goals.
tips:
The Ermita del Calvario in Cabeza del Buey is a very special place of worship, located on top of a hill. This hermitage offers stunning views of the surrounding landscape.
The route to the hermitage begins on Calle Oriente and winds along the slope of the Sierra del Pedregoso. Along the way, there are 14 crosses that symbolize the stops that Jesus made while carrying the cross before being crucified. The hermitage, built in the 18th century by the Gante family, has recently been restored and houses simple but beautiful paintings inside.

faqs.question: Is the Ermita del Calvario trail run suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered accessible for most fitness levels. While Komoot rates it as 'moderate' for jogging, the research indicates it's primarily an 'easy walk' with a mix of paved and natural paths. You'll need sure-footedness on some sections, but it's a good option for those new to trail running.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins on Calle Oriente in Cabeza Del Buey. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for street parking or designated areas within Cabeza Del Buey close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain. You'll encounter approximately 3.16 km of paved track, 1.61 km of unpaved track, 546 meters of natural path, and a small 102-meter section of asphalt. This mix provides a diverse running experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers exceptional panoramic views, especially from the Ermita del Calvario viewpoint. You'll see the extensive La Serena region, framed by the surrounding mountain ranges of Vegas-Altas, Siberia, and La Serena. On clear days, you might even spot the Montánchez, Villuercas, Torozo, and Cabras mountain ranges, as well as the nearby Andalusian region of Los Pedroches. The path also winds through typical local vegetation like cork oaks, gorse, and rockroses.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Beyond the Ermita del Calvario itself (an 18th-century hermitage), you'll pass the 'Cruz del Siglo XXI or del Milenio,' a large cross placed in 2000. Along the path, there are 14 Stations of the Cross. Keep an eye out for ancient, though degraded, cave paintings in a small cave near the end of the trail, and centuries-old elm trees around the Ermita.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I observe along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Calvario viewpoint is an excellent spot for birdwatching. You might see emblematic and endangered Iberian fauna such as imperial eagles, black storks, Egyptian vultures, griffon vultures, and black vultures.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly recommended for an evening run during summer. Its westward orientation provides some of the most beautiful sunsets in the region. Generally, the views are exceptional on clear days, making spring and autumn also great choices for comfortable temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Ermita del Calvario trail run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and varied terrain, and to practice Leave No Trace principles by cleaning up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Ermita del Calvario trail run from Cabeza Del Buey. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to the trailhead in Cabeza Del Buey?
faqs.answer: While Cabeza Del Buey is a town, specific public transport links directly to the Calle Oriente trailhead are not detailed. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: Based on the description, the route is primarily an out-and-back trail, leading from Cabeza Del Buey to the Ermita del Calvario viewpoint and back. There is no indication of it being a full loop.
